name: Koyl Yrenum, the Vizier
oracle_text:
Whenever another creature dies, you may pay {1}. If you do, look at the top two cards of target opponent's library. Put one into their graveyard and the other back on top.
When Koyl Yrenum dies, look at the top three cards of target opponent's library. Put one into their graveyard and the other two back on top.
Shroud.
flavor_text: Feretha still rules Denethix. Everyone knows this. Everyone.

validation_errors: Card is B but has "hexproof" which is primarily U/G
Card is B but has "indestructible" which is primarily W

design_notes: The Vizier who rules through deception and the orchestration of sacrifice. Koyl manipulates opponents' libraries through repeatable death triggers: whenever another creature dies, he peers into their deck for {1} per activation. When Koyl himself dies—the ultimate sacrifice of an expendable advisor—he gains a larger payoff (three cards instead of two), creating genuine mechanical tension between using him as a protected asset versus accepting his death for value. Shroud protects him from targeting effects (fitting black's evasion through hidden knowledge) while maintaining color pie integrity. This design reinforces the flavor: Koyl is both the orchestrator of others' demise and a creature whose own sacrifice yields significant advantage. The repeatable effect scales well with sacrifice outlets and death-heavy strategies, fitting the Deep Descent (UB control) archetype.
